name: MLAuditBench
description: MLAuditBench: An interactive RL environment for evaluating LLM agents on ML experiment integrity auditing. Contains 62 synthetic ML experiments across 3 difficulty tiers and 8 violation types (v1.1).
citation: MLAuditBench: An Interactive RL Environment for Evaluating LLM Agents on ML Experiment Integrity Auditing. Anonymous Authors, 2026.

distribution.name: environment-source
distribution.description: Source code for the MLAuditBench environment (supplementary material; public URL withheld for blind review)

distribution.name: experiment-templates
distribution.description: Base JSON templates for 5 ML experiment archetypes

recordSet.name: experiments
recordSet.description: 62 synthetic ML experiments across 5 archetypes with 8 violation types (v1.1)

field.name: violations
field.description: List of V1-V8 violation codes; empty list indicates clean experiment

field.name: red_herrings
field.description: List of red herring types (hard tier only)

recordSet.name: violation_taxonomy
recordSet.description: 8 violation types based on Kapoor-Narayanan (2023) taxonomy

rai:dataCollection: All experiments are synthetically generated from parameterized templates using a seeded random number generator. No real-world datasets, real paper artifacts, or personally identifiable information are included.
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: No missing data. All experiments are fully self-contained with preprocessing code, split configuration, training logs, and evaluation reports.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Violation injection is parameterized and reproducible from integer seeds. Preprocessing code, split configurations, and evaluation reports are generated by archetype-specific templates in generator.py.
rai:annotationsPerItem: Each experiment is labelled with ground-truth violation codes (V1-V8) and a clean/violated flag. Labels are deterministically derived from the generation process, not human-annotated.
rai:annotatorDemographics: Not applicable. Ground-truth labels are derived algorithmically from the experiment generation process.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. All experiment data is synthetic. The human evaluation study used ML-trained students under informed consent; no raw participant data is released.
rai:intendedUse: Benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on interactive ML experiment integrity auditing. Intended as a research tool; not for use as an automated publication gate.
rai:limitations: Violations are programmatically injected into synthetic experiments; real-world violations may be more subtle. Current archetypes cover sklearn-style pipelines only. Pool size (62 experiments) is smaller than static-dataset benchmarks.
